success_criteria: CI proves install import CLI and tests
Docs are honest about v0 maturity
Repo uses its own workspace model for real work
goals.title: Make Palari Company OS a professional Python CLI foundation

success_criteria: Work can move from scope to evidence to review to human decision
Future broker or policy work stays clearly out of scope until designed
goals.title: Grow the product model without bringing back old ticket ceremony

humans.name: Rafa Founder
humans.capacity_signal: Sets direction and accepts repo milestones

humans.name: Codex Maintainer
humans.capacity_signal: Historical records only; not available for new authority

palaris.name: Steward
palaris.scope: Keeps Palari Company OS maintainable, verifiable, and honest about maturity.

forbidden_actions: change authority policy without human decision
standards: prefer adaptive intensity over fixed ceremony
palaris.name: Architect
palaris.scope: Turns operating lessons into product model changes without adding ceremony for its own sake.

playbook_sources.label: Superpowers skills
playbook_sources.install_hint: Review the linked skill before use; Palari recommendations are guidance, not authority.

integrations.label: Palari Company OS GitHub repo
integrations.notes: Dry-run repo notification and issue/comment payload previews only.

integrations.label: Linear issue comments
integrations.notes: Created by palari linear post-gate for approved comment outbox sends.

workbenches.label: Repo Foundation
workbenches.summary: Maintainer work for the Python CLI, schemas, tests, docs, and local agent operating contract.

workbenches.label: Product Architecture
workbenches.summary: Design work for adaptive intensity, authority boundaries, broker safety, and future policy controls.

work_items.title: Complete strict validation history and repo hygiene foundation
work_items.scope: Represent the recent strict validation fixtures, append-only workspace history, professional metadata, CI, install smoke, and maturity documentation as one completed repo foundation milestone.
work_items.acceptance_target: Main contains the validation/history/hygiene commits, verification passes, GitHub CI passes, and the result is captured as dogfood history.

work_items.title: Add workspace init and seed ergonomics
work_items.scope: Create a small command or documented path for initializing a blank workspace without hand-writing the shell JSON.
work_items.acceptance_target: A new maintainer can create a valid empty workspace with one command and then add records through the CLI.

work_items.title: Split larger workspaces into maintainable source files
work_items.scope: Design and implement a simple multi-file workspace layout while preserving workspace.json compatibility and validation clarity.
work_items.acceptance_target: The dogfood workspace can grow without one giant JSON file and existing single-file workspaces still load.

verification_expectations: queue recommendation tests if model fields change
work_items.title: Design adaptive intensity for task sizing
work_items.scope: Turn the product idea of model-relative task sizing into concrete workspace fields and queue guidance without forcing subticket ceremony.
work_items.acceptance_target: Docs and model guidance explain when a stronger model can safely handle larger scoped work and when decomposition is required.

work_items.title: Define future broker and policy boundary roadmap
work_items.scope: Decide the minimum safety design before any real broker execution or policy acceptance can exist.
work_items.acceptance_target: Founder-approved design constraints exist before implementation starts.

work_items.title: Polish agent loop readiness surfaces
work_items.scope: Make the agent loop easier to follow by surfacing next-step types and missing-proof commands across CLI, dashboard, and docs.
work_items.acceptance_target: Agents can see the next safe step, missing proof commands, and handoff state without parsing raw attention text.

work_items.title: Add agent handoff packet contract
work_items.scope: Add a read-only agent handoff packet and route human review or decision states through it.
work_items.acceptance_target: Agents can hand off review or decision states with compact context while human action commands remain separate from agent-safe reads.

work_items.title: Clarify agent review and mode commands
work_items.scope: Make agent command surfaces clearer by exposing review-mode packet/check commands in detail/dashboard and making execute/review modes explicit in emitted commands and text output.
work_items.acceptance_target: Agents can tell whether they are in execute or review mode and can see the right packet/check command without guessing.

work_items.title: Harden dry-run integration validation
work_items.scope: Fail closed when hand-edited dry-run integration records declare unsupported provider actions, raw secret-looking plan payloads, or outbox payloads that differ from the approved plan.
work_items.acceptance_target: Workspace validation catches unsupported provider/action declarations, raw integration plan payload secrets, and outbox payload/source-boundary drift before any future execution boundary.

work_items.title: Clarify heuristic intensity warnings
work_items.scope: Make queue intensity wording distinguish declared work intensity from heuristic concerns so completed low-risk dogfood work does not look contradictory.
work_items.acceptance_target: Queue text labels heuristic mismatches as intensity concern or note and explains the declared intensity without changing safety behavior.

work_items.title: Enforce integration mode action boundaries
work_items.scope: Make integration mode authoritative so hand-edited dry-run integration records cannot declare actions outside their mode boundary.
work_items.acceptance_target: Workspace validation rejects mode/action mismatches while preserving dry-run provider previews and no live external calls.

work_items.title: Validate Palari memory source references
work_items.scope: Fail closed when a Palari memory source references a missing source record.
work_items.acceptance_target: Workspace validation rejects missing Palari memory source references while preserving existing examples and dogfood workspaces.

work_items.title: Add workspace data map command
work_items.scope: Add a read-only command that explains where workspace data, sources, integrations, memory references, and history live.
work_items.acceptance_target: Users and agents can run palari data map in text or JSON to understand data boundaries without live connectors or memory engines.

work_items.title: Allow safe receipt-ready work completion
work_items.scope: Let local low-risk receipt-ready work complete without forcing heavier evidence/review ceremony, while preserving dependency and external-write blockers.
work_items.acceptance_target: R1/R2 receipt-ready work with approval count 0, terminal dependencies, no open decisions, and no external writes can complete; other work still fails closed.

work_items.title: Add source readiness metadata
work_items.scope: Add optional source readiness metadata for data class, authority, steward, freshness, and redaction needs.
work_items.acceptance_target: Source readiness fields validate strictly, surface in data map, and preserve existing workspace behavior.

work_items.title: Expose source readiness in agent packets
work_items.scope: Carry source readiness metadata into agent brief packets so agents see data class, authority, steward, freshness, and redaction boundaries.
work_items.acceptance_target: Allowed source packets include readiness metadata, packet docs mention the boundary, and existing packet behavior stays stable.

work_items.title: Focus queue output on current work
work_items.scope: Make the queue command focus on current non-closed work by default while preserving an include-closed audit path.
work_items.acceptance_target: Default queue output omits closed items; --include-closed restores the full queue; docs and tests cover both paths.

forbidden_actions: give the Bash heuristic deny authority
let hook errors block unrelated sessions
work_items.title: Enforce packet write boundaries inside Claude Code
work_items.scope: Turn the cooperative agent check contract into structural enforcement for Claude Code sessions using PreToolUse, Stop, and SessionStart hooks driven by persisted claims and packets.
work_items.acceptance_target: Out-of-boundary Write/Edit/NotebookEdit calls are denied before the write; suspected Bash writes escalate to a human ask; stops are blocked while the tree escapes the boundary; install is idempotent and preserves foreign hooks.

work_items.title: Add two-minute onramp with init and work add
work_items.scope: Take an existing repo from nothing to an enforced Claude Code write boundary in three commands: init creates a starter workspace, work add creates an agent-startable bounded work item, and the current directory's workspace.json becomes the default workspace.
work_items.acceptance_target: init produces a valid starter workspace and refuses overwrites; work add yields a ready execute packet whose write boundary matches --write paths; the onramp works without --workspace flags.

work_items.title: Fix release workflow and surface Linear truthfully
work_items.scope: Fix the crashing changelog regex in the tag-triggered release workflow, add Linear to the README front page with honest live-write nuance, add a Linear smoke to verify.sh, and document the human PyPI trusted-publishing step.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: Declared outputs exist and verification passes.
verification_expectations: python3 - regex simulation of release notes extraction

work_items.title: Finish the Linear integration slice
work_items.scope: Complete the governed Linear loop: linear connect verifies credentials and prepares the integration record, linear issues lists team issues for discovery, linear sync pull-refreshes linked issues without webhooks, and status write-back flows through the same plan-approve-outbox-send gates as comments. Live-credential steps are recorded as a human decision blocker.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: All new commands work offline against fakes with tests; live calls only in connect/issues/sync/send paths; status updates require an approved plan; docs and public surface stay honest.

work_items.title: Publish local work to Linear with governed issue creation
work_items.scope: linear push WORK-ID plans a governed create_issue with the palari block embedded in the description; the existing approve/enqueue/send gates execute it and link the created issue back to the work item, so locally-defined tickets become phone-visible in Linear.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: push plans offline; send creates the issue via the client, stores external refs on the work item, and subsequent status write-back works on the pushed issue; already-linked work is rejected.

work_items.title: Create SWE-1.7 instructions for Devin usage
work_items.scope: Draft a Devin workflow with SWE-1.7 instructions for using the Palari Company OS repo.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: A .devin/workflows/palari-swe.md file is created with SWE-1.7 instructions and ./scripts/verify.sh passes.

work_items.title: Create GLM-5.2 instructions for Devin usage
work_items.scope: Draft a Devin workflow with GLM-5.2-specific instructions for using the Palari Company OS repo.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: A .devin/workflows/palari-glm.md file is created with GLM-5.2 instructions and ./scripts/verify.sh passes.

work_items.title: Improve SWE-1.7 and GLM-5.2 workflow instructions with full proof record steps and pre-completion checklist
work_items.scope: Update both workflow files to include full proof record steps, stale claim recovery as a normal step, and a pre-completion checklist gate.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: Both .devin/workflows/palari-swe.md and .devin/workflows/palari-glm.md are updated with complete proof record steps and pre-completion checklists. ./scripts/verify.sh passes.

work_items.title: Document RECEIPT_READY_REVIEW gate in both workflow files
work_items.scope: Add a note to both workflow files explaining the RECEIPT_READY_REVIEW gate: after closing out an attempt, the agent start may be blocked pending human review. lifecycle complete can still finalize the work item, but the full agent check/finish loop requires an active non-terminal claim.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: Both workflow files document the RECEIPT_READY_REVIEW gate and ./scripts/verify.sh passes.

work_items.title: Add verification failure recovery note to both workflow files
work_items.scope: Add a one-line verification failure recovery note to the pre-completion checklist in both workflow files.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: Both workflow files include verification failure recovery guidance and ./scripts/verify.sh passes.

work_items.title: Move workflow instructions to docs/agent and add pointers
work_items.scope: Move SWE-1.7 and GLM-5.2 instructions from .devin/workflows/ to docs/agent/, replace .devin/workflows/ files with thin pointers, and add a pointer from AGENTS.md.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: Instructions live in docs/agent/palari-swe.md and docs/agent/palari-glm.md, .devin/workflows/ files point to them, AGENTS.md references them, and ./scripts/verify.sh passes.

work_items.title: Add agent done shortcut for R1 work items
work_items.scope: Add a 'palari agent done WORK-ID --as PALARI-ID --json' command that auto-records a minimal attempt, receipt, closes out, and completes R1/light/0-approval work items in one step. For R2+ work, reject with a message directing to the full proof lifecycle. Update CLI parser, dispatch, output, tests, and docs.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: R1 work items can be completed with a single 'agent done' command. R2+ work is rejected with guidance. All existing tests pass and new tests cover the shortcut path.

work_items.title: Add git pre-commit hook for IDE-agnostic boundary enforcement
work_items.scope: Add palari git install/pre-commit/status commands. A pre-commit hook checks staged files against active claim write boundaries and rejects commits with out-of-boundary staged files. IDE-agnostic enforcement that works in any environment. Reuses path_allowed and active_claim_contexts from claude_hooks. Includes tests and docs.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: git pre-commit hook blocks commits with out-of-boundary staged files. palari git install installs the hook. palari git status shows hook state. All existing tests pass.

verification_expectations: python3 -m unittest tests.test_transition_checks tests.test_governance_completion tests.test_agent_packets tests.test_validation tests.test_path_policy tests.test_git_hooks tests.test_claude_hooks tests.test_agent_done
work_items.title: Harden model-independent governance and verification
work_items.scope: Mechanically bind review, evidence, human authority, scope, source, lifecycle, handoff, and verification state to exact current artifacts while preserving local-first minimality and compatibility through explicit migration where required.
work_items.acceptance_target: Every checkbox in docs/product/governance-hardening-contract.md has objective committed evidence; unsafe, stale, incomplete, contradictory, out-of-scope, non-independent, and mismatched states fail closed; focused and complete verification pass; an independent exact-head review recommends ACCEPT; founder authority remains pending.

work_items.title: Proof-Carrying AI Work Protocol v1
work_items.scope: Build a deterministic provider-neutral offline proof protocol, shared governance kernel, conformance corpus, and staged replayable journal without expanding external authority.
work_items.acceptance_target: PCAW v1 exports byte-identical bounded-work statements; offline verification fails closed on stale, contradictory, unsafe, or altered proof; journal continuity is replayable; complete verification and fresh exact-head review ACCEPT; founder authority remains human-only.

work_items.title: Asynchronous Approval Inbox, Approval Packs, and Reversible Checkpoints
work_items.scope: Implement immutable approval packs, exact batched human authority, parked execution, dependency-aware staleness, and content-addressed reversible checkpoints without external effects.
work_items.acceptance_target: One attributable human decision can authorize an exact eligible pack of 1, 10, or 100 individually proven items; mutations and dependency changes fail closed; checkpoint restoration is append-only and exact; complete verification and fresh independent exact-head review ACCEPT.
verification_expectations: python3 -m unittest tests.test_approval_packs tests.test_reversible_checkpoints tests.test_governance_journal tests.test_transition_checks tests.test_filesystem_security

work_items.title: Deterministic Agent Advance and Proof Reconciliation
work_items.scope: Replace manual post-implementation governance ceremony with a deterministic, idempotent agent-advance planner and executor that derives exact Git scope, runs bound verification profiles, records fresh proof, and stops at genuine review or human-authority boundaries.
work_items.acceptance_target: R1 work can complete and R2-R4 work can reach an exact review handoff through one safe advance command; no manual SHAs, changed paths, artifact hashes, proof commands, or claim release are needed; dry-run is fast; exact-head verification is safely reusable; complete acceptance verification and human authority remain intact.
verification_expectations: python3 -m unittest tests.test_agent_advance tests.test_agent_done tests.test_agent_packets tests.test_verification_profiles tests.test_governance_completion tests.test_transition_checks tests.test_filesystem_security

work_items.title: Repair GitHub CI Ruff failures for PR 12
work_items.scope: Repair the observed Ruff and mypy findings blocking PR 12 without changing runtime behavior, weakening assertions, or weakening CI.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: Ruff and mypy pass with all existing rules; focused and complete verification pass; GitHub PR 12 CI is green; only the eleven diagnosed source/test/script files plus governed workspace metadata change.

work_items.title: Make work identity and execution order-independent
work_items.scope: Make work-item identity opaque and concurrency-safe; expose explicit dependency DAGs; and make isolated parallel agent sessions mechanical without granting merge, push, review, or acceptance authority.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: New work defaults to collision-resistant opaque IDs; legacy IDs remain compatible; explicit dependency DAGs fail closed on missing, duplicate, self, and cyclic edges; independent work can be claimed in isolated Git worktrees without double-claiming; queue and diagnostics explain dependency and integration readiness without using ID order; focused, security, docs, install, and complete verification pass.
verification_expectations: python3 -m unittest tests.test_onramp tests.test_validation tests.test_agent_packets tests.test_cli_smoke

work_items.title: Make exact evidence portable across worktrees
work_items.scope: Allow exact artifact evidence created in an isolated Git worktree to verify from another linked worktree or clone without weakening path, commit, or repository identity checks.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: Evidence for the exact candidate verifies after safe worktree relocation; unrelated repositories, unavailable candidate commits, traversal, symlink escape, and altered bytes still fail closed; the blocked review for WORK-AC8CE9E4197C4D57B6FA096F14F33A3F becomes reviewable without manufacturing human authority.

work_items.title: Make routine governance converge to one human action
work_items.scope: Classify lifecycle friction, automatically resolve every safe deterministic transition, and compress eligible review, acceptance, and terminal bookkeeping into one exact human action without widening external or irreversible authority.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: Routine governed work converges idempotently to a true authority boundary; eligible reviewed work can be accepted and completed in one exact pack action; permitted human review-and-accept preserves separate bound records; stale, unsafe, external, irreversible, or identity-colliding states fail closed; closed work is terminal rather than blocked; affected and complete verification pass without material regression.
verification_expectations: python3 -m unittest tests.test_agent_advance tests.test_approval_packs tests.test_agent_packets tests.test_workspace_read_models tests.test_cli_smoke

work_items.title: Reach deterministic governance fixed points
work_items.scope: Add a bounded deterministic convergence driver that applies only already-authorized lifecycle transitions, tolerates governance-only proof commits, and stops at review, human authority, external state, terminal state, or a fail-closed error.
work_items.acceptance_target: Accepted current proof terminalizes automatically without a builder claim; repeated convergence is idempotent; governance-only commits do not stale proof; substantive post-proof changes, negative decisions, invalid evidence, loops, and iteration exhaustion fail closed; existing CLI/schema compatibility and full verification remain intact.

work_items.title: Review and integrate Claude's Palari blueprint
work_items.scope: Review Claude branch claude/repo-evaluation-7s05hw line by line, merge its consolidated blueprint, correct stale or overstated technical, market, privacy, legal, and implementation claims, and keep proposals subordinate to canonical repository contracts.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: The exact Claude branch is integrated as reviewed history; the blueprint accurately distinguishes shipped PCAW v1 from proposed v2 work, states trust and privacy limits, uses current primary-source evidence, and passes documentation and complete repository verification.
verification_expectations: git merge-base and exact branch-tip evidence

work_items.title: Support independent Palari reviews with human-only acceptance
work_items.scope: Allow a distinct declared Palari to perform an advisory exact-proof review while preserving human-only acceptance, legacy human-review compatibility, and fail-closed builder/reviewer independence.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: Review guides and bounded review packets expose typed Palari reviewers; only the matching non-builder Palari may record an agent verdict; PCAW verifies declared reviewer authority without treating agents as humans; human quorum remains human-only; the blueprint can receive a Steward review followed by one Founder decision.

focused refresh positive and unsafe-state negative tests

work_items.title: Refresh stale accepted proof without rewriting artifacts
work_items.scope: Superseded after independent review reproduced a final-window workspace race. Its changes-requested proof remains immutable; child WORK-C40EC756920849B59481E834D46E80F1 carries the bounded repair.

work_items.title: Close proof-refresh transaction race
work_items.scope: Bind proof reconciliation to the exact workspace state verified by the caller, fail closed on an injected final-window mutation, and preserve all existing proof, journal, scope, and human-authority behavior.
forbidden_actions: write outside the declared write paths
work_items.acceptance_target: A current-head proof refresh cannot record proof after any workspace mutation between verification and reconciliation; it returns REFRESH_STATE_CHANGED and writes no attempt, receipt, or evidence. Positive refresh and ordinary proof reconciliation remain compatible.
verification_expectations: focused injected-race negative and proof-refresh regression tests

attempts.result: Strict validation fixtures, append-only history, repository hygiene, install smoke, and CI were implemented and pushed to origin/main.
Added a workspace init command, tests, and documentation.
Added read-only split workspace collection_files support while preserving single-file workspaces.
Agent next, check, finish, and human-facing queue/state/detail/dashboard surfaces now share next-step classifications and prioritize missing proof commands.
Added read-only agent handoff packets and made agent next finish detail and dashboard surfaces point to the handoff bridge.
Clarified agent review and mode command surfaces for detail dashboard next check text output and review handoff text.

Hardened dry-run integration validation by sharing provider/action support, scanning plan payload previews for raw secrets, and requiring outbox previews and source boundaries to match approved plans.
Clarified queue intensity mismatch wording by separating declared intensity from heuristic concern and adding regression coverage.
Made integration mode enforce action boundaries, updated example modes, and added regression coverage.
Validated Palari memory source references, normalized example memory sources to source IDs, and added regression coverage.
Added read-only data map command covering storage, sources, integrations, memory-source references, history, and non-stored boundaries.
Allowed safe receipt-ready completion for local low-risk work while preserving dependency, decision, approval, and external-write blockers.
Added source readiness metadata, strict validation, data-map output, examples, and regression coverage.
Exposed source readiness metadata in agent brief packets and documented the boundary.
Changed queue CLI output to omit closed work by default and added --include-closed for audit views.
Added palari claude install/status/hook so Claude Code hooks deny out-of-boundary writes, block dirty stops, and inject the packet contract at session start.
Added palari init and palari work add so an existing repo reaches an enforced write boundary in three commands, with local workspace.json as the default workspace.

Fixed the crashing release workflow regex, added a tag/version guard, surfaced Linear on the README with honest live-write nuance, added a Linear smoke to verify.sh, and documented the PyPI trusted-publishing setup.
Completed the governed Linear loop: connect, issues, sync, and approval-gated status write-back, all tested offline against fakes.
Added linear push so Palari-born work items become governed, trackable Linear issues; send links external refs back in the same write.
Review and integration in progress.

tradeoffs: Fast demos are tempting but risky
Mock-only work preserves learning without side effects
Real authority requires custody and audit
decisions.question: What is the minimum safety design before Palari Company OS can execute broker actions or real policy authority?
decisions.context: The v0.1 foundation intentionally keeps broker execution and policy acceptance disabled.
decisions.recommendation: Keep broker and policy work documentation-only until signed gates, custody, audit, and human quorum are designed.
decisions.safe_default: Do not implement external side effects.

decisions.question: Authorize the live Linear connection: create a personal API key, export LINEAR_API_KEY, run linear connect, and optionally set LINEAR_WEBHOOK_SECRET for inbound sync.
decisions.context: The Linear integration slice (WORK-REPO-0021) is code-complete and fully tested against fakes. Live verification needs a real credential, which only a human may create. Palari stores only env:LINEAR_API_KEY, never the token value. This decision gates live verification only; the implementation work item is complete without it.
decisions.recommendation: Create a key at https://linear.app/settings/api scoped to your workspace, export LINEAR_API_KEY, then run: palari linear connect --json && palari linear issues --json
decisions.safe_default: Stay offline: every linear command except connect/issue/issues/import/start/inspect-block/sync/send works without a key.

decisions.question: Should scope expand for WORK-REPO-0027?
decisions.context:
Reason: GitHub CI reached mypy after the approved Ruff repair and reported 12 additional typing errors in these four files; green CI requires bounded type-only repairs without weakening mypy.
Requested write paths: src/palari_company_os/approval_packs.py, src/palari_company_os/verification_attestations.py, src/palari_company_os/agent_advance.py, src/palari_company_os/cli_dispatch.py
tradeoffs: Approving changes the declared work boundary.
Rejecting keeps the current attempt inside its packet.
Splitting preserves parallel safety for unrelated changes.
decisions.recommendation: Do not expand scope until a human reviews the new boundary.

useful: The CLI could author most records directly
The queue model cleanly identifies evidence review decision and integration states
Install smoke exposed a real Python 3.10 compatibility bug before closeout
failed: Long list fields through CLI flags are awkward
Review findings cannot yet be structured through first-class CLI flags
follow_up_needed: Consider import/export or seed helpers for larger workspaces
Keep dogfood workspace updated as repo maturity work continues
model_worker_notes: Dogfooding was feasible with ordinary CLI commands after seeding the empty workspace shell
outcomes.summary: The repo reached a cleaner v0.1 foundation with verification, install smoke, CI, and honest maturity docs.
outcomes.what_happened: The recent validation, history, and hygiene work was merged to main and then represented inside the dogfood workspace.
outcomes.what_changed: Palari Company OS now has a real internal workspace that can track its own next repo maturity work.

payload_preview.title: Add agent done shortcut for R1 work items
payload_preview.description:
Add a 'palari agent done WORK-ID --as PALARI-ID --json' command that auto-records a minimal attempt, receipt, closes out, and completes R1/light/0-approval work items in one step. For R2+ work, reject with a message directing to the full proof lifecycle. Update CLI parser, dispatch, output, tests, and docs.

Acceptance: R1 work items can be completed with a single 'agent done' command. R2+ work is rejected with guidance. All existing tests pass and new tests cover the shortcut path.

Palari work item: WORK-0007

```palari
{
  "goal": "GOAL-REPO-0001",
  "palari": "PALARI-STEWARD",
  "risk": "R2",
  "intensity": "standard",
  "scope": "Add a 'palari agent done WORK-ID --as PALARI-ID --json' command that auto-records a minimal attempt, receipt, closes out, and completes R1/light/0-approval work items in one step. For R2+ work, reject with a message directing to the full proof lifecycle. Update CLI parser, dispatch, output, tests, and docs.",
  "acceptance_target": "R1 work items can be completed with a single 'agent done' command. R2+ work is rejected with guidance. All existing tests pass and new tests cover the shortcut path.",
  "parallel_policy": "independent",
  "allowed_resources": [
    "AGENTS.md",
    "docs/agent/repo-map.md",
    "docs/agent/common-workflows.md",
    "docs/agent/contracts-and-invariants.md",
    "docs/agent/verification.md",
    "docs/product/agent-contract.md",
    "docs/product/agent-loop-smoke.md",
    "docs/product/command-reference.md",
    "src/palari_company_os/cli_parser.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/cli_dispatch.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/cli_output.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/agent_checks.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/agent_finish.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/models.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/agent_done.py",
    "tests/test_agent_done.py"
  ],
  "allowed_sources": [
    "SOURCE-REPO-FOUNDATION"
  ],
  "output_targets": [
    "src/palari_company_os/cli_parser.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/cli_dispatch.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/cli_output.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/agent_done.py",
    "tests/test_agent_done.py",
    "docs/product/command-reference.md",
    "docs/product/agent-contract.md"
  ],
  "forbidden_actions": [
    "write outside the declared write paths"
  ],
  "verification_expectations": [
    "./scripts/verify.sh",
    "python3 -m unittest discover -s tests",
    "./bin/palari validate --json"
  ],
  "conflict_targets": [
    "src/palari_company_os/cli_parser.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/cli_dispatch.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/cli_output.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/agent_done.py",
    "tests/test_agent_done.py",
    "docs/product/command-reference.md",
    "docs/product/agent-contract.md"
  ]
}
```
payload_preview.note: Team id is resolved live at send time; an empty team_key uses the only visible team.

payload_preview.title: Add git pre-commit hook for IDE-agnostic boundary enforcement
payload_preview.description:
Add palari git install/pre-commit/status commands. A pre-commit hook checks staged files against active claim write boundaries and rejects commits with out-of-boundary staged files. IDE-agnostic enforcement that works in any environment. Reuses path_allowed and active_claim_contexts from claude_hooks. Includes tests and docs.

Acceptance: git pre-commit hook blocks commits with out-of-boundary staged files. palari git install installs the hook. palari git status shows hook state. All existing tests pass.

Palari work item: WORK-0008

```palari
{
  "goal": "GOAL-REPO-0001",
  "palari": "PALARI-STEWARD",
  "risk": "R2",

IDE-agnostic enforcement that works in any environment. Reuses path_allowed and active_claim_contexts from claude_hooks. Includes tests and docs.",
  "acceptance_target": "git pre-commit hook blocks commits with out-of-boundary staged files. palari git install installs the hook. palari git status shows hook state. All existing tests pass.",
  "parallel_policy": "independent",
  "allowed_resources": [
    "AGENTS.md",

    "src/palari_company_os/cli_parser.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/cli_dispatch.py",
    "src/palari_company_os/cli_output.py",
```
